title: Fix: add sound in generated video
body:
Amazing project!!!
I found a small bug where the generated video does not have the sound of the original video.

Explore the source code to identify the files and functions to modify.

step_plan.summary: After producing MP4s from frames, update LivePortraitPipeline.execute to call merge_audio_video (ffmpeg-based) to merge the original driving video's audio into the generated video(s), and return the audio-containing file paths (with safe fallback to the silent files if merging fails).

steps.operation: Modify LivePortraitPipeline.execute to merge the driving video's audio into the generated mp4(s)
steps.reason: images2video currently writes silent MP4s from frames. To preserve the driving video's audio, call the existing merge_audio_video helper (which uses ffmpeg) after images2video produces the silent files. This ensures the final returned/served outputs contain the original audio track.
steps.side_effects: Behavioral/API: The method will return paths to audio-merged files (new filenames), which downstream callers (e.g., the Gradio UI) will start serving instead of the original silent files — confirm callers expect updated paths. Tests: Add/modify tests to assert that when driving input is a video with audio, the returned path points to a file containing audio; also test fallback behavior when merging fails. Dependencies: Requires ffmpeg to be available at runtime; if ffmpeg is missing or merge_audio_video fails, ensure code falls back to the original silent MP4s and logs the failure rather than raising unhandled exceptions. File-system: merged files will be additional outputs (or replacements) — ensure temporary/cleanup policy is handled. Performance: merging is an extra subprocess step that will increase processing time. Backwards compatibility: If other code expects the original filenames (without _audio suffix), they may be impacted — keep naming predictable and document change.
